rationale: In the citing paper, Trendel et al. (2018) appears only in a grouped citation in Study 4b to support that an alphanumeric memory task is a standard cognitive-load manipulation in marketing research. The paper does not engage the cited article's core theory or findings on warning effectiveness and implicit attitudes, nor does it assign a distinct role to this specific source. Its function is generic methodological background rather than a paper-specific building block.
